ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Continued To Jun-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for and with Janet Rhodes, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Amanda Ebey for Christine Byrd, Petitioner/Objector (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew for Bennett Hong, Special Administrator (via Zoom).

Parties reached a settlement agreement, that the letters need to be extended, and that they will file for final distribution soon after the letters get approved for extension. All matter are continued to June 23, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Fajardo. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-16-2025 Continued To Apr-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for and with Janet Rhodes, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Amanda Ebey for Christine Byrd, Petitioner/Objector (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew for Bennett Hong, Special Administrator (via Zoom).

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 24, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by April 7, 2026. The matter is continued to April 21, 2026 and the Parties are to return on April 21, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

All matter are continued to April 21 , 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge:Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Continued To Jul-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for and with Janet Rhodes, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Seth Skootsky for Christine Byrd, Petitioner/Objector (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew for Bennett Hong, Special Administrator (via Zoom).

The court inquired as to the status of the case and whether the parties were interested in mediation. Mr. Skootsky reported that discovery was ongoing and that subpoenas had been issued to various third-party entities, including healthcare providers. Partial responses had been received, and follow-up subpoenas had been issued to expedite production. The parties agreed that additional time was needed to obtain and review outstanding discovery before determining next steps. They anticipated that, upon return, the matter would either proceed to depositions, mediation, or potentially trial setting, depending on the information obtained.

The parties jointly requested a continuance and that the letters of special administration be extended. The Court extended the letters of special administration and continued the matter to July 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Continued To Apr-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Justin Hedemark, Esq., for Janet Rhodes, Petitioner.
Seth Skootsky, Esq., for Christine Boyd, Petitioner.
Christopher Jew, Esq., for Bennett Hong, proposed Special Administrator.

At the outset, the court confirms with all counsel present that there is a stipulation to appoint Mr. Hong as Special Administrator. Therefore, the court confirm the appointment of Bennett Hong as Special Administrator with General Powers and Full IAEA Authority. A Bond in the amount of $720,000 is required. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate Referee. The proposed order is executed in open court. At the court's query, counsel offers an update as to their respective, competing petitions.

After discussion, the subject petitions are continued to Apr-29-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Continued To Jan-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for Janet Rhodes, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Seth Skootsky for Christine Boyd, Petitioner/Objector (via Zoom).

Counsel reported ongoing discussions about appointing a special administrator to protect estate assets, particularly the decedent's home, given recent storms. Both parties agree on the need for a special administrator but are discussing who should serve in that role.

Counsel requested a 45-day continuance to allow further discussions and potential resolution.

The court granted the continuance and set the next hearing for January 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court advised that if an agreement is reached on the special administrator, the appropriate filings should be submitted in advance for approval.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Justin Hedemark appeared by zoom for Janet Rhodes (petitioner).
Seth Skootsky appeared by zoom for Christine Marie Boyd (heir).

This matter was set for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. Mr. Skootsky appeared to object. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to October 15, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Skootsky is to file his objections by October 1, 2024. If the objections are not filed by then, they will be deemed waived.
